volunteer_history.where_and_when: I began volunteering regularly in college around 2010 at various campus and community organizations in Portland, Oregon, including food drives and neighborhood clean-ups. Since moving to Seattle in 2016, I have consistently volunteered with education- and housing-focused nonprofits, most notably with Rainier Valley Community Learning Center (2017–2020) and Evergreen Family Shelter (2020–2024). I typically commit to at least one ongoing weekly volunteer role per year, plus several one-off events such as fundraising runs, book drives, and community fairs.
volunteer_history.current_or_most_recent_your_title: Evening Homework Club Tutor & Program Assistant (Volunteer)
volunteer_history.current_or_most_recent_duties_line_1: Provided twice-weekly after-school homework support and academic mentoring to 8–12 elementary and middle school students living in transitional housing, focusing on reading comprehension, basic math skills, and building consistent study habits.
volunteer_history.current_or_most_recent_duties_line_2: Assisted the program coordinator with setting up and closing the homework room, tracking student attendance and progress in a shared spreadsheet, communicating with parents and shelter staff about student needs, coordinating small volunteer teams (3–4 volunteers per shift), and developing simple enrichment activities (STEM games, journaling prompts, and read-aloud sessions) tailored to children experiencing housing instability or trauma.
volunteer_history.current_or_most_recent_reason_for_leavin: Relocated to a different part of the city and started a new full-time job with longer evening hours, making it difficult to reliably attend the weekday homework sessions.

I discussed my changing schedule with the volunteer coordinator in early 2024, and we agreed that it would be better for the students to have a tutor who could commit to being there every week. I stayed on through the end of the school year (June 2024) to help transition my responsibilities, document the routines I had established for the reading groups, introduce the new volunteers to the students, and ensure that the children had continuity and did not feel abruptly abandoned. I remain on their mailing list and have offered to come back for occasional weekend events when my schedule allows.
volunteer_history.volunteer_history_duties_2_line_1: Supported an evening English-as-a-Second-Language (ESL) class for adult learners by circulating during small-group and pair activities, helping students practice conversation, pronunciation, and basic writing tasks such as filling out forms, drafting simple work emails, and preparing for job interviews.
volunteer_history.volunteer_history_duties_2_line_2: Assisted the lead instructor with classroom logistics, including preparing handouts, setting up audio equipment, taking attendance, and recording student participation notes; provided individualized support to learners with very limited literacy; occasionally led warm-up exercises and conversation circles focused on real-life topics like visiting the doctor, talking to a child’s teacher, or navigating public transportation; and offered informal tech help using translation apps and email on students’ phones when appropriate.
volunteer_history.volunteer_history_reason_for_leaving_2_l: In-person classes were suspended in March 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and I was unable to continue regularly once the program shifted to a fully virtual model.

When the center moved classes online, many of the students I worked with did not have stable internet access or were sharing devices with multiple family members. My own work responsibilities also increased significantly when my employer transitioned to remote operations, and I was logging unpredictable hours. I helped the instructor for a few weeks with phone check-ins and basic Zoom orientations, but I could not commit to the full virtual schedule. After talking with the staff, we decided to formally end my weekly volunteer role while leaving the door open for future in-person opportunities, which eventually led me to shift my focus to the homework club at Evergreen Family Shelter once in-person youth programs resumed.
volunteer_history.volunteer_history_duties_3_line_1: Welcomed clients to the pantry, conducted brief intake screenings to verify eligibility based on income and household size, and helped families select appropriate food items while maintaining dignity, privacy, and respect for cultural and dietary needs.
volunteer_history.volunteer_history_duties_3_line_2: Restocked shelves, sorted and checked donations for quality and expiration dates, assembled emergency food boxes for delivery to homebound seniors, assisted with weekly inventory counts, and helped train newer volunteers on pantry procedures, food safety basics, and how to de-escalate stressful situations when clients were anxious or frustrated due to long wait times or limited supplies.
volunteer_history.volunteer_history_reason_for_leaving_3_l: Moved from Portland, Oregon to Seattle, Washington for a new full-time job opportunity and could no longer attend weekly shifts at the pantry.

During my final month, I coordinated with the volunteer manager to gradually hand off my regular Thursday evening shift and update some of the intake instructions I had been helping to refine, so that newer volunteers would have clear written guidance. I left in good standing and have stayed in occasional contact with a former fellow volunteer through social media; I also still donate annually to support the pantry’s holiday food drive even though I no longer live in the area.

work_history.work_history_duties_1_line_1: Analyzed customer behavior and operational data for mid-sized nonprofit and education-sector clients, building dashboards and recurring reports in Tableau and Power BI to help them track program outcomes, donor engagement, and internal performance metrics; regularly cleaned and merged data from multiple systems (CRM, email marketing, and event registration platforms) using SQL and Python.
work_history.work_history_duties_1_line_2: Collaborated with cross-functional teams (account managers, software engineers, and client success staff) to translate non-technical client questions into clear analytical projects, presented findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, documented methodologies and data dictionaries, mentored two junior analysts on best practices for data quality and visualization design, and contributed to internal process improvements such as standardizing report templates for nonprofit clients.
work_history.work_history_reason_for_leaving_1_line_1: Accepted a new position with a smaller mission-driven analytics firm that focuses exclusively on supporting education and housing nonprofits, aligning more closely with my long-term career and volunteer interests.
work_history.work_history_reason_for_leaving_1_line_2: After five years at North Sound Data Solutions, I felt that I had grown significantly in my technical skills but wanted to move into a role where the majority of my client work directly supported organizations addressing educational equity and housing stability—the same areas where I have been volunteering. I discussed my goals with my manager during my annual review in early 2024, and when an opportunity arose at a specialized social-impact analytics firm, I applied and was offered the role. I provided over a month of notice, documented my active projects in detail, trained my replacement on key client accounts, and stayed on call for questions during the first two weeks after my departure to ensure a smooth transition for the clients I had worked with for several years.
work_history.work_history_duties_2_line_1: Supported senior researchers on program evaluation projects for local governments and nonprofits by conducting literature reviews, cleaning and coding survey data in SPSS and Excel, helping design questionnaires and interview protocols, and assisting with the preparation of draft reports and slide decks for client presentations.
work_history.work_history_duties_2_line_2: Coordinated logistics for focus groups and stakeholder interviews (scheduling, reminder calls, room setup, and note-taking), contributed to basic descriptive analyses and visualizations, maintained organized project files and version control for research instruments, and communicated regularly with clients to clarify data questions and follow up on missing information while adhering to confidentiality and data protection standards.
work_history.work_history_reason_for_leaving_2_line_1: Relocated from Portland to Seattle and sought a more advanced, analytics-focused role after completing several multi-year evaluation projects.
work_history.work_history_reason_for_leaving_2_line_2: By mid-2019, I had reached a natural transition point on two major projects that were wrapping up, and I had also decided to move to Seattle to be closer to family and pursue broader career opportunities. My supervisors were aware of my relocation plans several months in advance, and we coordinated my departure so that final reports and data handoffs were completed before my last day. They provided strong references that helped me secure my subsequent position at North Sound Data Solutions, and we remain in professional contact for occasional consulting questions and networking.
